decisions.reasoning: Incineroar is a bulky pivot that will likely switch out or use Fake Out; U-turn allows me to deal chip damage while maintaining momentum and bringing in a more favorable matchup.

Tornadus-Therian is at a disadvantage against Incineroar due to Intimidate and the threat of Flare Blitz. Switching into my own Incineroar allows me to neutralize the opponent's Incineroar with Intimidate and pivot safely.

Calyrex-Ice is at 39% HP and will be outsped and KO'd by Incineroar's Flare Blitz next turn. Switching to Tornadus-Therian provides a safe pivot to preserve Calyrex-Ice for later while avoiding the immediate KO.

Tornadus-Therian is at 46% and will be KO'd by Flare Blitz next turn, while Hurricane is only a 3HKO. Switching to my own Incineroar allows me to Intimidate the opponent and safely pivot or check their physical attackers.
Calyrex-Ice has fainted, so I must send in a new Pokemon. Tornadus-Therian is a safe pivot to scout the Incineroar's next move and maintain momentum.
I outspeed Incineroar and Hurricane does significant damage (up to 53.5%). Since I am at 80% HP, I can survive a Flare Blitz and guarantee a 2HKO on the following turn while maintaining momentum.

Tornadus-Therian is at 22% and will be KO'd by any move from Incineroar, but Hurricane has a high chance to KO the 19% HP Incineroar. Since I outspeed, I should take the guaranteed opportunity to remove the opponent's Incineroar before fainting.

Tornadus-Therian is at 22% and will be OHKOed by Wild Charge; switching to Flutter Mane provides an immunity to Iron Hands' Fighting-type moves and forces the opponent to respect the speed and offensive pressure.
Flutter Mane is already fainted; I must send in Tornadus-Therian to handle the Iron Hands, as it resists Fighting-type moves and can pivot out or threaten with Hurricane.
Tornadus-Therian is guaranteed to be OHKOed by Wild Charge, and since Incineroar provides Intimidate support and resists Ice Punch, it is the safest pivot to handle Iron Hands.
